next_steps: Perform ultrasound-guided or direct endoscopic biopsy of the nasopharyngeal mass
Send biopsy tissue for histopathology, microbiology (fungal/bacterial culture), and flow cytometry
Order ANCA (PR3 and MPO) testing and serum IgG4 levels
Perform full-body PET/CT to evaluate for systemic involvement and identify optimal biopsy sites
Urgent consultation with Rheumatology and Hematology/Oncology
Avoid empiric treatment until a tissue diagnosis is established
Perform ultrasound-guided or direct endoscopic biopsy of the nasopharyngeal mass for histopathology, microbiology (fungal/bacterial/mycobacterial culture), and flow cytometry
Order serologic workup: ANCA (PR3 and MPO), serum IgG4 levels, and ACE/lysozyme
Order infectious disease screening including QuantiFERON-TB Gold, Histoplasma/Blastomyces serologies, and beta-D-glucan
Perform chest/abdomen/pelvis PET/CT to evaluate for systemic involvement and identify additional sites for potential biopsy
Perform urgent ophthalmology consultation for comprehensive evaluation
Obtain MRI/MRCP of the abdomen to evaluate the hepatobiliary tree for evidence of IgG4-related sclerosing cholangitis given abnormal liver enzymes
Avoid empirical immunosuppressive or antifungal therapy prior to securing a histopathological diagnosis
